q: What do OpenAPI extensions add to a custom connector?
o: Advanced behaviors like dynamic values, dynamic schema, test connection and chunking
why: OpenAPI extensions enable dynamic values/schema, test connection and chunk transfer.

q: Which editor lets you edit the connector's underlying definition directly?
why: The Swagger editor edits the connector's OpenAPI (Swagger) definition.

q: Decide whether each OpenAPI-extension statement is true.

rows.s: Extensions are expressed within the OpenAPI definition.
The Swagger editor exposes the raw definition for editing.
Extensions require a plug-in to function.

why: Extensions live in the OpenAPI definition (editable in the Swagger editor); no plug-in needed.

q: Enabling chunk transfer on an operation helps with...
o: Handling large payloads by transferring them in chunks
why: Chunk transfer lets large content be sent/received in chunks.

q: What does the 'dynamic list of values' extension do?
o: Populate a parameter's selectable options at runtime from the API
why: Dynamic values fetch selectable options at runtime instead of hardcoding them.

q: Configuring dynamic values requires pointing to...
o: An operation that returns the list, and the value/display fields
why: You reference an operation returning the list and map its value/display properties.

q: Decide whether each dynamic-values statement is true.

rows.s: Dynamic values improve UX by offering real options instead of free text.
They are configured through OpenAPI extensions.

why: Dynamic values give real, current options and are set via extensions.

q: What is the purpose of the 'test connection' extension?
o: Let the platform validate a connection is working
why: Test connection defines how the platform verifies a connection is functional.

q: The 'dynamic schema' extension is useful when...
o: The response/request shape varies based on the operation or inputs
why: Dynamic schema adapts the payload structure to the chosen operation/inputs.

q: Dynamic schema differs from dynamic values in that it...
o: Adapts the data STRUCTURE, not just a list of selectable values
why: Dynamic values populate options; dynamic schema shapes the payload structure itself.

q: Decide whether each dynamic-schema statement is true.

rows.s: Dynamic schema can reflect an operation-specific response shape.
It is configured through an OpenAPI extension.
It is the same as chunk transfer.

why: Dynamic schema (via an extension) tailors payload structure; chunking is unrelated (large transfers).

q: Not all APIs support every extension. What should you verify first?
o: The API's support/behavior for the extension you plan to use
why: Extension behavior depends on API support; confirm compatibility before relying on it.

q: In the exercise, extensions are added after which step?
o: Importing the solution/connector to work with
why: You import the solution/connector, then add and configure the extensions.

q: Configuring dynamic values for an 'invoice type id' parameter means users will...
o: Choose from a live list of invoice types instead of typing an id
why: Dynamic values present the real invoice types to pick, improving accuracy/UX.

q: Decide whether each exercise statement is true.

rows.s: You can add a test connection extension to validate connectivity.
Extensions are configured per operation/parameter as needed.

why: You add test-connection and configure dynamic values/schema per operation/parameter.

q: Which combination of extensions most improves a data-entry action's usability?
o: Dynamic values for pick-lists plus dynamic schema for the payload
Chunk transfer plus a new theme
Test connection plus a Power BI visual
why: Dynamic values (real options) and dynamic schema (right structure) together improve data entry.
